_A_CONFIRM_COMPONENT_DESCRIPTION_: AConfirm is a confirmation dialog API built on top of the modal system. Use AConfirmAPI to open a confirm window, pass content and button settings, and optionally control loading state, focus return, or external closing.
_A_CONFIRM_GROUP_BASIC_DESCRIPTION_: The simplest setup: open a confirm dialog with a header, message, and save handler.
_A_CONFIRM_GROUP_BUTTONS_DESCRIPTION_: This example customizes button labels and opens the confirm dialog in a larger size.
_A_CONFIRM_GROUP_FOCUS_DESCRIPTION_: Use selector-close-ids to return focus to specific elements after the confirm dialog is closed.
_A_CONFIRM_GROUP_ASYNC_DESCRIPTION_: AConfirm can update its state after opening. Here the first example toggles loading during an async save, while the second closes the dialog from outside through the event bus.
_A_CONFIRM_EXAMPLE_BASIC_MODAL_BODY_: Do you want to publish the current changes now?
_A_CONFIRM_EXAMPLE_BUTTONS_MODAL_BODY_: This action will move the project to the archive and hide it from the active list.
_A_CONFIRM_EXAMPLE_FOCUS_MODAL_BODY_: After closing this dialog, focus returns to one of the configured buttons.
_A_CONFIRM_EXAMPLE_ASYNC_MODAL_BODY_: Click save to simulate an async request and show the loading state.
_A_CONFIRM_EXAMPLE_TIMEOUT_MODAL_BODY_: This confirm dialog will close automatically after a short timeout.
